message:
fix(patterns): sandbox_resolve_dir propagates canonicalize errors except NotFound

The sandbox-path resolver at crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs:1215
was silently swallowing every canonicalize() failure by returning
Ok(None):

    match candidate.canonicalize() {
        Ok(...) => ...,
        Err(_) => return Ok(None),  // <- collapses all failures
    }

The existing comment explained the intent: a DANGLING symlink should
be treated as "nothing to load" rather than erroring, because the
attack surface here is read-side and a dangling link can't exfiltrate
anything. That reasoning is correct for NotFound specifically — but
the match arm was wider than the reasoning. Other canonicalize()
failure modes that legitimately mean "this pack directory is
malformed" were collapsed into the same silent-skip path:

  * symlink loops (ELOOP / "too many levels of symbolic links")
  * permission denied on an ancestor (EACCES)
  * filesystem-level errors (EIO, stale NFS handles)
  * Windows-specific path-too-long failures

All of those are signals the operator should see — either it's their
own misconfiguration OR it's a hostile path trick (e.g. a symlink
loop that confuses other loaders). Silently treating them as "no
packs configured" masks both.

## Fix: narrow the silent-skip branch to NotFound only

    match candidate.canonicalize() {
        Ok(...) => ...,
        Err(e) if e.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound => return Ok(None),
        Err(e) => {
            return Err(PatternError::InvalidRule(format!(
                "pack directory {} is not accessible: {e}",
                candidate.display()
            ))
            .into());
        }
    }

The NotFound branch preserves the dangling-symlink "skip cleanly"
behavior that was the documented intent. Every other error surfaces
through PatternError::InvalidRule with the candidate path in the
message so the operator knows which pack location is the problem.

## Regression test (Unix-only, cfg(unix) gated)

`sandbox_resolve_dir_symlink_loop_errors` constructs a temp root with
a self-referential symlink `.ft/patterns -> patterns`, calls
sandbox_resolve_dir on it, and asserts:
  - `expect_err(...)` fires (no longer silently Ok(None))
  - error message contains one of "not accessible", "Too many levels
    of symbolic links", or "filesystem loop" — covering macOS / Linux
    phrasings of ELOOP that propagate through canonicalize().

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
